attempts.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
    
        The image of the scene has been provided.
        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Put the books on the desk.
    

SCENE CAPTION:
The scene contains a bedroom or study area with two primary surfaces: a bed and a desk. The instruction is to "Put the books on the desk," which implies the books are currently not on the desk and must be moved there.

**Objects and Types:**

- **Books (object)**: There are two books visible. Both are rectangular with illustrated covers featuring purple and blue floral or landscape imagery. They are currently resting on the bed.
- **Laptop (object)**: A dark gray laptop is open and placed on the bed, near the books. It is not a receptacle.
- **Panda Plush Toy (object)**: A white and black panda plush toy is sitting on the bed. It is not a receptacle.
- **Bed (receptacle)**: The bed, covered with an orange, pink, and black geometric patterned comforter, is acting as a surface holding the books, laptop, and panda toy. It is a receptacle because it is currently containing multiple objects.
- **Desk (receptacle)**: A wooden desk with a smooth, light brown surface is positioned in the room. It is currently holding a tablet (or smartphone) and a small, translucent white bowl. It is a receptacle.
- **Chair (object)**: A wooden chair with a metal frame is tucked under the desk. It is not a receptacle.
- **Floor (object)**: The floor is covered with a dark gray, textured carpet.

**Quantities:**

- Books: 2
- Laptops: 1
- Plush Toys: 1
- Desks: 1
- Chairs: 1
- Tablets/Smartphones: 1
- Bowls: 1

**Spatial Relationships:**

- The two books are **inReceptacle** the bed.
- The laptop is **inReceptacle** the bed.
- The panda plush toy is **inReceptacle** the bed.
- The tablet and the bowl are **inReceptacle** the desk.
- The chair is positioned adjacent to and partially under the desk.
- The bed is located in the foreground, while the desk is in the background, to the right of the bed from the viewer's perspective.

**Current State Relevant to Planning:**

- The books are not currently on the desk; they are on the bed.
- The desk has available space to receive the books, as it is not full.
- The robot is not currently at the location of any object. It must first **GotoLocation** the bed to pick up a book, then **GotoLocation** the desk to place it.
- No objects are being held by an agent (the robot) at this time.
- No objects are in a state of being clean, hot, cool, sliced, or toggled, as these predicates are not relevant to the current task of moving books.

**Visual Properties:**

- The bed’s comforter has a vibrant, maze-like pattern in shades of orange, pink, and black.
- The books have distinct, colorful covers that make them easily identifiable.
- The desk is a simple, modern design with clean lines and a light wood finish.
- The panda plush toy is large and sits upright, adding a playful element to the scene.
- The lighting in the room is soft and even, with no harsh shadows, suggesting an indoor setting with ambient light.

The task requires the robot to move the two books from the bed to the desk, which involves navigating to the bed, picking up each book, and then placing them on the desk.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem put_books_on_desk)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        book1 book2 - object
        bed desk - receptacle
        laptop panda_plush - object
        tablet bowl - object
        chair - object
    )
    (:init
        (atLocation robot bed) ; Assuming robot starts near the bed for efficiency, as it's the source.
        (inReceptacle book1 bed)
        (inReceptacle book2 bed)
        (inReceptacle laptop bed)
        (inReceptacle panda_plush bed)
        (inReceptacle tablet desk)
        (inReceptacle bowl desk)
        (not (holdsAny robot))
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle book1 desk)
        (inReceptacle book2 desk)
    ))
)
```
attempts.problem:
(define (problem put_books_on_desk)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        book1 book2 - object
        bed desk - receptacle
        laptop panda_plush - object
        tablet bowl - object
        chair - object
    )
    (:init
        (atLocation robot bed) ; Assuming robot starts near the bed for efficiency, as it's the source.
        (inReceptacle book1 bed)
        (inReceptacle book2 bed)
        (inReceptacle laptop bed)
        (inReceptacle panda_plush bed)
        (inReceptacle tablet desk)
        (inReceptacle bowl desk)
        (not (holdsAny robot))
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle book1 desk)
        (inReceptacle book2 desk)
    ))
)
attempts.plan:
(gotolocation robot book2)
(pickupobject robot book2)
(gotolocation robot desk)
(putobjectinreceptacle robot book2 desk)
(gotolocation robot bed)
(pickupobject robot book1)
(gotolocation robot desk)
(putobjectinreceptacle robot book1 desk)

final.response:
```pddl
(define (problem put_books_on_desk)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        book1 book2 - object
        bed desk - receptacle
        laptop panda_plush - object
        tablet bowl - object
        chair - object
    )
    (:init
        (atLocation robot bed) ; Assuming robot starts near the bed for efficiency, as it's the source.
        (inReceptacle book1 bed)
        (inReceptacle book2 bed)
        (inReceptacle laptop bed)
        (inReceptacle panda_plush bed)
        (inReceptacle tablet desk)
        (inReceptacle bowl desk)
        (not (holdsAny robot))
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle book1 desk)
        (inReceptacle book2 desk)
    ))
)
```

    (:objects

        book1 book2 - object
        bed desk - receptacle
        laptop panda_plush - object
        tablet bowl - object
        chair - object
    )
    (:init
        (atLocation robot bed) ; Assuming robot starts near the bed for efficiency, as it's the source.
        (inReceptacle book1 bed)
        (inReceptacle book2 bed)
        (inReceptacle laptop bed)
        (inReceptacle panda_plush bed)
        (inReceptacle tablet desk)
        (inReceptacle bowl desk)
        (not (holdsAny robot))
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle book1 desk)
        (inReceptacle book2 desk)
    ))
)
